text: When you’re writing code, bugs are unavoidable. The first step in fixing them is identifying the bug—this means understanding what the program should do versus what it actually does. A systematic approach is key: reproduce the problem consistently, then isolate the part of the code causing it. Many programmers use debugging tools like breakpoints, which pause execution so you can examine variables step by step. Another simple but powerful technique is logging—adding print statements at different points to track values and see where things go wrong. Logging can reveal hidden patterns or unexpected data. With patience and a clear method, debugging becomes less frustrating and more like solving a puzzle.
